dependencies: Limited number of communications satellites available for transmission of programming
Dependence on satellite systems to transmit broadcast and cable networks to affiliates
Reliance on third-party MVPDs for distribution
Dependence on third-party measurement services like Nielsen for audience ratings
risks: Failure or destruction of satellites or transmitter facilities could materially adversely affect business
FCC reallocation of C-Band satellite transmission spectrum reducing availability
Disruption of satellite transmissions due to local disasters, extreme weather, power outages, terrorist attacks, cyberattacks
Limited number of communications satellites available
Potential further reallocation of remaining C-Band spectrum for commercial wireless services

regulatory_risks: FCC regulations governing television broadcasting and station licensing
National television ownership rule limiting audience reach to 39% with UHF discount
Foreign ownership restrictions limiting non-U.S. ownership to 25% without FCC waiver
Must-carry and retransmission consent regulations
Children's programming requirements and commercial limitations
FCC content regulations prohibiting obscene material and restricting indecent/profane material
Closed captioning and audio description requirements
FTC regulations on advertising practices and disclosures
State legislation on AI disclosure in political advertising
ATSC 3.0 broadcast transmission standard transition and simulcast requirements
Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA)
Video Privacy Protection Act (VPPA)
Telephone Consumer Protection Act
Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Consumer Privacy Rights Act (CPRA)
State privacy laws in Virginia, Colorado, Connecticut, Utah and over a dozen other states
EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
UK, Canada, Australia, China, Mexico data protection legislation
U.S. Department of Justice rule on transfer of U.S. sensitive personal data to countries of concern
Data breach notification legislation in all 50 states
Truth-in-Lending Act, Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Fair Housing Act, RESPA for Credible operations
Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act
SAFE Act mortgage licensing requirements
State insurance licensing laws
FCC dual network rules prohibiting common ownership of major broadcast networks
Political advertising regulations and record retention requirements
Emergency Alert System compliance requirements
Potential changes to national ownership cap and UHF discount
FCC review of foreign ownership compliance in license renewal applications
Potential elimination or modification of retransmission consent rules

sanction_risks: Ongoing tensions with China, North Korea, Russia and other nation states may lead to cyberattacks
Conflicts in the Middle East and Europe may lead to cyberattacks or actions negatively impacting systems
Nation state-sponsored cyberattacks and activities pose increasing risks to systems
